context: Tests whether the agent correctly identifies and applies the three key scoring dimensions with their weights (Completeness 40%, Conciseness 30%, Actionability 30%), correctly identifies the missing 'Use when...' clause as the highest-impact fix, recommends executable code over abstract prose, and flags known-concept content that reduces conciseness.

checklist.name: Completeness weight correct
checklist.description: Plan states that Completeness has the highest weight (40%) among the dimensions

checklist.name: Use when clause highest impact
checklist.description: Plan identifies adding a 'Use when...' clause as the highest-priority or highest-impact change

checklist.name: Use when quantified
checklist.description: Plan states that the missing 'Use when...' clause adds approximately +15% to overall score (or is flagged as the most valuable single change)

checklist.name: Revised description includes Use when
checklist.description: The revised description block in the output includes a 'Use when...' clause (e.g. 'Use when you need to retrieve, rotate, or audit secrets...')

checklist.name: Executable code recommended
checklist.description: Plan recommends replacing abstract prose instructions with executable code examples (for actionability)

checklist.name: Known concepts flagged
checklist.description: Plan identifies that the existing prose explains things an agent already knows (e.g. 'don't hardcode secrets', 'use environment variables') as reducing Conciseness

checklist.name: High-impact first ordering
checklist.description: Quick wins are ordered so the highest-impact item (Use when clause) appears first

checklist.name: Dimension coverage
checklist.description: Plan covers all three main scoring dimensions (Completeness, Conciseness, Actionability) with separate assessments
